reviews.review_description:
I loved Thumper and decided to give this game a shot based on the good reviews.  

I'll cut right to the chase--this game plays like a free web-based flash game from 10 years ago.  There's nothing immersive about it--no reason for it to be in VR.  

It's a hollow shell mannequin of Thumper's aesthetic, which has audio queues telling you something epic is happening when nothing could be further from the truth.  

I really hope people see this review because this game is a pathetic cash-grab and I don't want others to feel as cheated as I do right now.

Sadly I played this game for about 20 minutes and haven't picked it up in weeks.  Because of this I can't return it.

Got this with Quest+. I really like it. Relaxing, yet tension building. In a good way though. Although I got it free, I paid for their other game, Thumper, based on liking Thrasher so much. Thumper is much harder. I enjoy it less
Thrasher is worth the purchase. Kinda my warm up game at this point.
Just wish it was a bit longer & drawn out. Maybe 15 levels of 3 vs. 9.

Looking for the perfect 20 buck game to make you get bored amd creeped out from the first 30 seconds? Well lucky you, cause Thrasher exists!
Avoid at all costs, except if you only like sitting,moving your hands left and right and going through circles. !NOT RECOMMENDED!

3 stars because It is good polished game for what it do, but what it does is boring, the playing itself in the game is very boring to me. You have like a glowing rope that you connect it to glowing circles and they move in nice movement but the whole play itself is boring.

This game might be for some, but personally I found it so dull I was done with it in less than 10 minutes.   Even if it turns out that for some people moving a centipede with their hands through circles is fun, I can promise no one sane would consider it worth the asking price.  It's the quality of a free game, or 2.99 at most.

I assume most of the reviews are fake, which makes me wonder about some of the other games too.

I love this game. It works sort of like a wave shooter, but it’s not a wave shooter. It is arcade-like. You control this eel that snakes through the environment, to basically “ clear the board” so to say, by different mechanics that are added into the game as a progresses.  It’s quite original. The game also looks beautiful, and it takes advantage of VR very well. I highly recommend it. ￼ I like this a lot more this developers other game called Thumper ￼
It has visuals that are far from stunning and the game itself gets boring very quickly. Glad I didn't have to buy it.
There is nothing to do. No story. Cheap visuals.

reviews.review_title: These guys know how VR should work
reviews.review_description: I love this game from the first second. It's very meditative but challenging at the same time and very physical somehow.

reviews.review_title: This is what VR is made for!
reviews.review_description: Thrasher is like a classic 1970’s 1980’s psychedelic arcade game that you played in a dream. Now it’s real and in virtual reality. The graphics are so clear, and gameplay is smooth. Happy this has leaderboards and achievements. The only thing that would make it better is if they added a chill freeplay mode where you simply eat otherworldly creatures and explore, instead of fast paced it would be cozy

After several months of quest+ release failures it looks like its time to cancel soon if meta can't get it together...  how about some value as a trade off for our monthly payment? Maybe something that wouldn't be in the bargain bin at a dollar store?
Nothing here to make me want to keep playing.
This should be the vr
GO LITTLE DRAGON I BELIEVE IN YOUUUUUU 🐉🐉🐉

So easy to trip out with this app. 

Visually stunning, but not very exciting so I'm giving it 4 stars.
Become one with the cosmic-psychedelic-space-kite-thing 👾

reviews.review_title: Takes time but gets really fun
reviews.review_description: I really had a hard time at first with this one and could only play for small sessions but I kept coming back and getting more comfortable with it feels very satisfying and the creature starts to just move where you think it should , it almost feels like moments of singularity. Very challenging and I recommend breaks but I think it’s a glimpse of what the future of gaming can and will be. The music and gameplay evolves very slowly but at a wonderful pace. The best hand tracking experience to date for me. Well done.

I sat on the fence for a long time before trying Thumper and when I eventually did it blew my mind.

What I've played so far of Thrasher is definitely living up to expectations both visually and aurally. It feels very much in the same universe as its predecessor, yet fresh and original in it's own way.

I'm on a Q2 using the controllers rather than hand tracking and I'm liking the simplicity of the 'whip' movement mechanic. It feels very precise for now however I fully expect it to become more challenging to control as the levels increase in intensity.

So if you liked Thumper it's probably a no-brainer to pick this up. Also reminds me a bit of maybe Rez Infinite or Squingle but otherwise there aren't many comparable 'psychedelic' games like this in VR and for that reason alone it’s worth trying.

Update: I’ve noticed for some time that my review doesn’t appear to be visible on the meta store. Don’t know why. I can only see it if I’m logged in to my account. What’s that all about eh? Edit: Oh now I can see it lol :)

Beautiful game. If you are an arcade score chaser this game won’t let you down. I saw in another review that it’s too slow and too easy. It begins slow because it needs to build a foundation for what you are supposed to be doing but once it builds that I would say it is the opposite of slow and easy

But the game feels way too forgiving in the first levels. While hitting forbidden red objects comes with a time penalty, it is way too simple to clear the levels with plenty of time to spare. I have not lost a single life in this game so far. Where is the challenge?

The German game localization needs work. Returning to the main menu was translated like requesting a restaurant menu. Really?

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback!  We will continue to tune the challenge of the game in the early levels.  We are also in the process of updating the game's localization text, look out for that soon.

reviews.review_description: Like Thumper, this is a great game to sit on the couch and get completely sucked into. It’s challenging and addictive. And of course the music and atmosphere is top notch. You won’t be disappointed.

Easy to jump into and a fun challenge to master. 
The beginning levels start you off easy but keep progressing and you'll find new elements to keep the game interesting.
The graphics are smooth. The controls work intuitively and flawlessly with controllers or hand tracking. Some of the best hand tracking I have experienced.
Be sure to pick this polished gem up for your collection!

First off, the gameplay, visuals, and music are 5/5, if you are on the fence, just buy the game. It’s hypnotic, psychedelic, and addicting. Initially I was thinking it was too simple, but it does feel like the opposite side of the same coin in regards to Thumper. If Thumper is all about precision with very little room for error, Thrasher is all about improvisation and flow, more forgiving until you start chasing those high score. I docked a point because the sound and music are so incredible, but it feels like very muffled, like it was compressed or rendered poorly. I put on headphones expecting crisp and full spectrum audio, but it really felt like something was missing, the highs aren’t there and lows didn’t boom. I think this could easily be fixed, and I hope it does.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words and feedback :)  We are looking into improving the audio mix both with and without headphones in a future update

reviews.review_description: Once you understand how the scoring works, the game truly begins. And Brian, never stop being an artist.
Next Level game!
Awesome!!

went for thumper then upped to the bundle with this in a whim only to discover this is a markedly better quest experience!

Creative and feels native instead of like a bad port from controller. Hope it’s on an eye tracking platform one day!

reviews.review_title: Great game: issues with stuttering fixed
reviews.review_description: I'm no longer having issues with stuttering, this game is really fun. Bumping my review to 5 stars!

developer_response.body: Thanks!! We've been working hard (and continue to work hard) on performance.  Since launch, we've bumped the framerate to 90 on Quest 3/3S, increased the render resolution on all Quest headsets and reduced stuttering significantly.  We appreciate everyone's support :)

reviews.review_description: If you loved Thumper, you'll like this. More amazing art and music.
Just finished a cosmic dreamscape session of mind melting beauty and sound. Phew!

There's nothing else out there that's really like this. Beautiful, immersive, challenging, addictive.

l i n k t r . e e / q u a c k b i r t h
Fantastic!!
